videos.transcript: Hi guys welcome to another episode of Ultimate Bucket List, and on today's show I show you around one of the most interesting and cost-effective winter-sun destinations that you can visit Antalya, the jewel of southern Turkey. this is famous for its cultured city, its historical sites, and the many Las Vegas style resorts that span each of its beaches. With warm year-round temperatures and its location on the Mediterranean, this is the perfect winter sun destination and the best part is it's cheap. Very cheap! A holiday here won't break the bank but more on that later. Probably the first stop in our tour of Antalya is this Kaleici or the old town. This is the original part of Antalya and even though the weather was a bit crappy on the day that I actually went into town, it doesn't detract from the fact that there's a lot of things to see. But be warned; it's like a bit of a labyrinth so whilst you're wandering down all these side streets and back streets having a look at all these old buildings and old shops, it's pretty easy to get lost. So I highly recommend that you download an offline map and keep it on your phone just in case you can't find your way out. It really is kind of like a labyrinth in here albeit a very interesting one. Probably the most famous bit of Antalya is this the Yivli Minare Mosque. The tower itself is the symbol of Antalya, kind of like what the Eiffel tower is to Paris, but significantly smaller. You can see this tower from miles away and especially out at sea. But the tower is actually attached to the town's mosque and the mosque itself, you can actually go inside. It's ornately decorated on the inside and it's pretty interesting to walk around but bear in mind remember to take your shoes off before you enter the building. Whilst you're around the mosque you'll find one of the many museums that adorn the city. I went into this one it looks pretty interesting especially some of the artefacts on display. Once again shoes off before you actually go inside. But the main attraction of this particular museum is a whole bunch of dead guys.
